extraction.one_line_summary: VONDERPOEL Mathieu won the uphill sprint finish at Visegrád after GHEBREIGZABHIER Biniam crashed EWAN Caleb in final meters through wheel contact
tactical_patterns: Alpecin-Fenix controlled chase into finale despite no pure sprinter, positioning VONDERPOEL perfectly for uphill finish at 4% gradient
KEMNA Leonard attacked solo inside final 3km on uphill approach, forcing sprint teams to chase without organized leadout trains
Crashes at final right-hand corner before climb eliminated sprint trains, making positioning at turn more valuable than traditional leadout

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: survived climb on GHEBREIGZABHIER's wheel but touched wheels while sprinting uphill, suggesting positioning over power deficiency
rider_signals.GHEBREIGZABHIER Biniam: positive: positioned second wheel on 4% uphill sprint despite no team support, demonstrating pure climber-sprinter capability
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: positive: picked up 4 bonus seconds for third after crash, unexpectedly competitive on uphill sprint finish despite GC classification
rider_signals.KEMNA Leonard: positive: solo late-race attack as non-favorite from Bora-Hansgrohe disrupted sprint preparation, nearly held 10-second gap inside 2km
